parsed_review.summary: CP00-259 is a descriptor-only RP07 search-core permission-binding-slice pack (risk B, 40 units, RP07.P06.M04.S24-RP07.P06.M06.S13). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claim is honest and supported: manifest and command-evidence report production_ready=false / implemented_pending_review (no enterprise-trust overclaim). Pack binding, requirements, contract current_pack, plan pack, and manifest all agree on scope; deliverable distribution (3/18/7/7/4/1) and micro-title distribution (2/25/13) match the baseline exactly. All 7 required public exports exist across service.js/validators.js and surface via index.js wildcard re-exports. The CP259 code is pure frozen-object construction: freezeCp259Result forces every runtime/dispatch/write/audit/storage flag false, rows are descriptor_only with runtime_execution=false, and no I/O, fetch, fs, exec, or DB was introduced — so the no-write/no-real-data boundary holds. The descriptor validator's assertions all resolve against ROW_EXTRAS inherited through CP256-CP258 and against the no-write attestation (permission_bypass_detected, promotes_claude_to_final_approval, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation all false). The validator script and 3 model tests assert coverage.valid, slice.valid, deny-over-allow enforced, no bypass, human_final_approval_required, claude_is_final_approval=false, read_only=true, and handoff to CP00-260. Authority boundary is preserved throughout (Claude read-only, not final approver; human final approval required). CP178+ review hardening is encoded via forbidden_review_evidence (auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing-field rejections) and a hardened_review_sequence that runs Claude before adjudication/inspection/closeout; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. No P0/P1/P2 findings; one P3 informational note about post-review artifacts that are produced after this review by design. This review does not constitute final approval.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts referenced but not yet present at review time (by design)
findings.details: manifest.json evidence_refs and required_pack_artifacts (lines 1648-1661) list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-259/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, none of which exist yet in the pack directory (only manifest.json and command-evidence.json are present). This is expected and correct: command-evidence.json notes[3] documents these as post-review gated outputs produced from the accepted review (normalize/receipt/adjudication/inspection steps of the hardened_review_sequence), and command-evidence reports production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review. It is therefore not a defect in the implementation under review, but the pack cannot complete final closeout until those artifacts are generated and the final gated commands (closeout-pack:review:run/normalize/receipt:validate, closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) pass.
findings.recommendation: No code change required. The human final approver should confirm the hardened review sequence generates claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json and that all post-review gated validation commands pass before the CP00-259 closeout commit.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as by-design / non-blocking; tracked for the deterministic post-review closeout steps outside this read-only review.
